items.title: Enhance auto-recharge deployment in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update ensures the production auto-recharge CronJob is created during PlatformBackend deployments by applying the necessary manifest. The CronJob now correctly points to the AceDataCloud PayBackend, enabling successful payment processing. Manual verification confirmed that the updated configuration works as intended, improving the reliability of the auto-recharge feature.

items.title: Enhance Account Deletion Process in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the account deletion process to preserve user data while disabling future logins. Key identifiers are anonymized but historical data remains intact, ensuring compliance with privacy guidelines. The changes include updates to the user model and associated tests to validate the new behavior.

items.title: Enhance user skills parsing for Python 3.8 compatibility in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses a critical crash in the AuthBackend production rollout by ensuring that user skill frontmatter parsing is compatible with Python 3.8. Key changes include modifying the `user_skills.py` file to implement a fallback for the `frontmatter` import and adding a new unit test to validate the parsing functionality. These improvements enhance stability and maintain functionality across different Python versions.

items.title: Enhance saved card retrieval for Setup Intents in Pay Backend 🎉
items.summary: This update improves the retrieval of saved card details when accessing Stripe SetupIntents. By expanding the payment method during the retrieval process, the system now returns essential card metadata including card brand and last four digits, enhancing the user interface experience after a successful setup.

items.title: Enhance Account Deletion Process in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the account deletion flow by implementing soft deletion and PII anonymization for users. The changes ensure that authenticated users can safely delete their accounts while retaining necessary audit information. Additionally, the AuthBackend now rejects tokens from inactive or deleted users, enhancing security.

items.title: Enhance Auto-Recharge Clarity in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the auto-recharge feature by clarifying the display of pending card configurations. Key changes include better labeling for application columns, distinct indicators for missing cards, and actionable prompts for users to add a card when necessary. These enhancements aim to streamline user experience and reduce confusion.

items.title: New Automated Corporate Bank Transfer Implementation in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a comprehensive implementation plan for automated detection and recharge of corporate bank transfers, consisting of 11 documents and approximately 1.7k lines. The goal is to enable enterprise clients to automatically match pending orders and complete balance recharges without manual intervention after transferring funds to the platform's corporate account.

items.title: Enterprise P0 Technical Design for Governance and Billing in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a comprehensive technical design document for the enterprise-level governance, audit, and invoicing features. Key components include enhancements to budget governance, the creation of a unified audit log, and the implementation of billing and credit limit functionalities. The document outlines specific changes to the Django models and API contracts, ensuring a robust framework for B2B transactions.

items.title: Enhance Auto-Recharge Confirmation Flow in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update fixes the auto-recharge saved-card confirmation process by ensuring the confirmed `setup_intent_id` is passed correctly after a successful Stripe SetupIntent. It also cleans up unconfirmed pending configurations to prevent stale states that could block retries. Key changes include updates to the request body and improvements in user experience during card setup.

items.title: Enhance auto-recharge config handling in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update ensures that newly-created auto-recharge configurations remain inactive until the Stripe card setup is confirmed. It also replaces stale unconfirmed configurations instead of blocking retries, improving the user experience during payment setup. Key modifications were made in the auto_recharge_config.py file to implement these changes effectively.

items.title: Enhance iOS Account Deletion with Typed Confirmation in Nexior 🔒
items.summary: This update strengthens the account deletion process for iOS users by introducing a strict typed confirmation requirement. The 'Delete account' option is now exclusive to iOS, featuring a detailed warning and a requirement for users to type their username before proceeding. Additionally, internationalization support has been added for 17 languages, ensuring a consistent user experience across different locales.

items.title: New Account Deletion Flow in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new account deletion feature on the user profile page, ensuring compliance with App Store guidelines. Users can now delete their accounts with a strict confirmation process that requires typing their username, preventing accidental deletions. The feature also includes internationalization support for 17 languages.

items.title: Fix consent checkbox text overflow in Frontend Web 🎉
items.summary: This update addresses the issue of the auto-recharge consent checkbox text overflowing outside the create dialog. A new class has been added to allow the checkbox label to wrap properly within the dialog's width, enhancing the user experience. Additionally, text sizing and line-height have been scoped for better styling control.

items.title: Enhance Auto-Recharge App Selection with Search and Grouping in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces three key UX improvements to the application select on the auto-recharge console. Users can now filter applications by name, while global-scope applications are prioritized at the top with a clear 'General Balance' label. Additionally, localized group labels have been added across multiple languages to enhance usability.

items.title: Fix auto-recharge issues and enhance Stripe integration in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves multiple issues on the auto-recharge page, including fixing the application dropdown and package selection. It also addresses the Stripe card input problem by adding a fallback for the publishable key. These changes improve user experience and ensure proper functionality in production.

items.title: Introducing Stripe Auto Top-Up Configuration Page in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update adds a new feature-flagged console page for Stripe auto top-up configuration, allowing users to bind applications and saved cards for automatic recharges. Key changes include a new router route, menu item, and operator for managing auto-recharge configurations, along with support for multiple languages. The implementation ensures a seamless user experience while maintaining compliance with Stripe's requirements.

items.title: Empower Users with Self-Service Account Deletion in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new backend endpoint allowing authenticated users to delete their own accounts via a DELETE request to `/api/v1/users/me`, addressing Apple App Store requirements. The permission model has been adjusted to ensure that only the account owner can perform this action, while superusers retain the ability to delete any account. Comprehensive tests have been added to validate the functionality and security of this feature.

items.title: Resolve iOS App Store Rejection Issues in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses critical issues that led to the rejection of the iOS app submission. Key changes include gating unresponsive 'Top Up' buttons on iOS, enhancing error messaging for Apple Sign In failures, and adding an in-app account deletion feature. These improvements ensure compliance with App Store guidelines and enhance user experience.
